items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap

items.blurb: The 'Three-point leaders by NBA season' quiz — The NBA instituted the three-point line during the 1979-80 season. How many of the league leaders in three-pointers made for every season since then can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Jason Kidd wants Cooper Flagg to do just one thing after rookie season
items.blurb: Jason Kidd wants Cooper Flagg to do just one thing after rookie season — To say that the Dallas Mavericks' season was a disappointment would be a bit of an understatement, but Flagg provided a bright spot.

items.headline: Clippers make NBA history with remarkable turnaround
items.blurb: Clippers make NBA history with remarkable turnaround — A team that started the season with big expectations and a veteran group of future Hall of Famers finishing as the No. 9 seed is disappointing. However, the Clippers even being in the play-in tournament is actually an historic turn around based on where they were early in the season.

items.blurb: Anthony Davis Wants Championship Plan From Wizards Before Committing Long-Term — Anthony Davis made clear Monday that the Washington Wizards must present a credible path to contention this summer, setting up what figures to be one of the NBA offseason's most consequential front office conversations. items.headline: Azzi Fudd Selected No. 1 In WNBA Draft By Dallas Wings
items.blurb: Azzi Fudd Selected No. 1 In WNBA Draft By Dallas Wings — Former UConn star Azzi Fudd was selected first overall by the Dallas Wings in the 2026 WNBA Draft, earning a $500,000 salary under the league's newly ratified collective bargaining agreement, nearly seven times what teammate Paige Bueckers earned as the top pick in 2025. items.headline: Hawks choose first-round matchup with Knicks on season's final day
items.blurb: Hawks choose first-round matchup with Knicks on season's final day — With a win on the final day of the NBA season, the Atlanta Hawks could have clinched fifth place in the East and a first-round matchup with the Cleveland Cavaliers. Instead, they sat their starters and let fate — and the Boston Celtics — decide their playoff seed.

items.blurb: Pistons end regular season on historic note with win over Pacers — In their final game of the regular season, the Detroit Pistons made their 2025-26 campaign historic no matter what happens in the playoffs.

items.headline: Report: Pelicans to commence search for new head coach
items.blurb: Report: Pelicans to commence search for new head coach — The New Orleans Pelicans are opening a search for a new head coach, NBA insiders Marc Stein and Jake Fischer reported on Monday.

items.headline: Luka Doncic Returning To Los Angeles From Spain Before Start Of Playoffs
items.blurb: Luka Doncic Returning To Los Angeles From Spain Before Start Of Playoffs — Luka Doncic is returning to Los Angeles on Friday after spending the past week in Spain receiving multiple treatments on his left hamstring strain. Doncic is rehabilitating a Grade 2 left hamstring strain. The Los Angeles Lakers haven't provided a timetable for when Doncic may be able to return from his injury. The Lakers o

items.headline: LeBron James, Brandon Ingram Named NBA Players Of Week 25
items.blurb: LeBron James, Brandon Ingram Named NBA Players Of Week 25 — LeBron James and Brandon Ingram have been named the NBA's Players of the Week for Week 25. James averaged 24.0 points, 6.0 rebounds and 9.7 assists as the Los Angeles Lakers went 3-1. James stepped into a bigger role with Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves out with injury. James and Michael Jordan are the only players to win the award while in

items.blurb: Pacers' Tyrese Haliburton discusses painful two-month shingles battle behind recent appearance change — Fans who were poking fun at Haliburton for apparent weight gain should cut him a break. He's battling a painful medical problem.

items.blurb: Four NBA coaches with their jobs on the line in the playoffs — Twenty teams survived the NBA regular season to reach the playoffs or play-in tournaments. That doesn't mean their coaches have job security.

items.headline: Immanuel Quickley Day-To-Day With Mild Hamstring Strain After MRI
items.blurb: Immanuel Quickley Day-To-Day With Mild Hamstring Strain After MRI — The Toronto Raptors announced Monday that medical imaging has diagnosed guard Immanuel Quickley with a mild hamstring strain. Quickley is considered day-to-day. Quickley averaged 16.4 points, a career-high 5.9 assists, 4.0 rebounds and 31.9 minutes in 70 games (all starts) this season. items.headline: Tyrese Haliburton says shingles will test him this off-season, not Achilles recovery
items.blurb: Tyrese Haliburton says shingles will test him this off-season, not Achilles recovery — Indiana Pacers star Tyrese Haliburton was prepared to spend this past NBA season in the shadows, fighting his way back from a torn right Achilles tendon. He never anticipated the long journey back would take a detour because of a bout with shingles.

items.headline: NBA PER (Final 2025-26): Giannis, Jokic, Shai, Wembanamya Comprise Top-4
items.blurb: NBA PER (Final 2025-26): Giannis, Jokic, Shai, Wembanamya Comprise Top-4 — Giannis Antetokounmpo finished the 2025-26 regular season with the NBA's best PER with a mark of 32.1 ahead of Nikola Jokic, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Victor Wembanyama and Luka Doncic. Antetokounmpo played in just 36 games as he dealt with a number of injuries during the Milwaukee Bucks' disappointing season. Final 2025-26

items.blurb: Pre-Playoff Betting Favorite Has Won 70 Percent Of NBA Championships Since 1980 — The Oklahoma City Thunder open the 2026 NBA playoffs as championship favorites at +110 , carrying a 48 percent implied probability. items.headline: Six key matchups to watch in Raptors-Cavaliers series
items.blurb: Six key matchups to watch in Raptors-Cavaliers series — There’s little to gather from the Raptors’ regular-season sweep of the Cavaliers since it was before the addition of James Harden. Handling the savvy vet is one of six factors Zulfi Sheikh dives into that could determine the four-five series.

items.blurb: ‘They can have a good chance’: Wall gives Raptors’ keys against Cavaliers — Former NBA All-Star and current NBA on Prime analyst John Wall joins The Raptors Show and discusses the Toronto Raptors’ first-round series against the Cleveland Cavaliers and which Play-In Tournament matchup he is looking forward to the most.

items.blurb: How will Raptors’ lack of playoff experience impact them vs. Cavaliers? — The Raptors Show hosts Matt Bonner and Blake Murphy break down whether playoff experience really matters and, if so, how it will ultimately affect the Raptors as they take on the Cleveland Cavaliers in the first round of the NBA Playoffs.

items.headline: Bucks’ poor finish raises questions about Antetokounmpo’s future
items.blurb: Bucks’ poor finish raises questions about Antetokounmpo’s future — The postseason is starting without the Milwaukee Bucks for the first time in a decade. How soon Milwaukee gets back to the playoffs could depend on an offseason that’s shaping up to be among the most critical in franchise history due to Giannis Antetokounmpo’s uncertain future.

items.blurb: Will West come down to three-horse race between Thunder, Spurs and Nuggets? — VSiN By The Books’ Jensen Lewis and Marc Malusis previews the NBA’s Western Conference from a betting standpoint, and why they see the title contention coming down to a three-horse race between the Thunder, Spurs and Nuggets.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Uncertain About Bucks Future, Won't Rule Out Extension
items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo Uncertain About Bucks Future, Won't Rule Out Extension — Giannis Antetokounmpo delivered conflicting signals about his future with the Milwaukee Bucks following a season-ending 126-106 loss to the Philadelphia 76ers on Sunday. items.headline: Dillon Brooks: Good to go for Play-In Game
items.blurb: Dillon Brooks: Good to go for Play-In Game — Brooks (hand) isn't listed on the injury report ahead of Tuesday's Play-In Game against Portland. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Grayson Allen: Iffy for Tuesday — Allen (hamstring) is questionable for Tuesday's Play-In Tournament matchup against the Trail Blazers. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Jalen Green: Good to go vs. Portland — Green (knee) has been cleared to play in Tuesday's Play-In Game against the Trail Blazers. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
